archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Phase-Change Front Propagation & Boil-Off Gas (BOG) Suppression Controller for LNG Carrier Sloshing Dampening
domain: Offshore LNG Carrier Navigation & Cryogenic Cargo Management
description: This system orchestrates the redistribution of Liquid Natural Gas (LNG) within partially filled membrane tanks during rough seas to prevent destructive sloshing impacts while simultaneously managing the boil-off gas pressure through predictive thermal modeling. Used by Chief Officers and automated ballast control systems, it is technically demanding because it requires fusing real-time inertial measurement unit (IMU) data with complex computational fluid dynamics (CFD) approximations to react within milliseconds to prevent structural failure of the containment membranes.
rules: MUST adhere to the IACS (International Association of Classification Societies) Unified Requirements for LNG Carriers regarding sloshing impact pressure limits.
MUST interface with legacy PLCs using Modbus TCP or CANopen to control fuel gas supply valves and ballast water pumps within a deterministic 50ms cycle time.
MUST utilize the ISO 80000 standards for thermodynamic variables when calculating enthalpy changes during the phase shift from liquid to gas.
MUST implement a dual-redundant architecture compliant with IEC 61508 SIL 3 (Safety Integrity Level) for all critical control loops.
MUST ingest and process high-frequency data streams from distributed fiber-optic temperature sensors embedded in the tank insulation layers.
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely on asynchronous HTTP REST APIs for control commands, as the network latency variability in satellite-linked offshore environments will cause catastrophic lag in tank stabilization.

DO NOT use generic machine learning models trained on static datasets, as the fluid dynamics of sloshing are non-linear and require real-time physics-based simulation updates based on current sea state.
DO NOT decouple the safety shutdown logic from the primary control loop, as any disconnection in the fail-safe chain violates maritime SOLAS (Safety of Life at Sea) regulations.
key_technical_challenges: Modeling the chaotic multiphase flow interactions of LNG and its vapor within a moving reference frame with millimeter-precision accuracy.
Synchronizing sensor fusion data from accelerometers, gyroscopes, and fiber-optic temperature arrays occurring at different sampling rates (1kHz vs 10Hz) without jitter.
Executing a complex stability optimization algorithm on edge hardware with limited thermal dissipation in a hazardous (explosive gas) environment.
Predicting the 'sloshing pressure front' trajectory 2-3 seconds ahead of time to preemptively adjust ballast levels before impact peaks.

recommended_stack.language: Rust - Provides memory safety guarantees while delivering the deterministic performance required for real-time control systems in safety-critical environments.
recommended_stack.framework: FFI bindings with C++ CFD libraries (like OpenFOAM) - Necessary to leverage existing, validated fluid dynamics solvers without re-implementing them.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB - Optimized for high-write throughput of time-series sensor data with precise retention policies for voyage compliance reporting.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Stream for real-time event buffering and back-pressure management between sensor ingestion and the control loop.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ for deterministic, low-latency publish-subscribe communication between the edge controller and the ship's bridge display terminals.
special_services: Predictive Boil-Off Gas (BOG) Generation Rate Estimator

compliance_flags: SOLAS Chapter II-1 (Construction - Structure, Subdivision and Stability, Machinery and Electrical Installations)
IGC Code (International Code for the Construction and Equipment of Ships Carrying Liquefied Gases in Bulk)
IEC 61508 (Functional Safety of Electrical/Electronic/Programmable Electronic Safety-related Systems)
